steps.title: Identify the triangle sides
steps.content: Triangle $QRS$ is a right triangle with the right angle at $R$. Side $SR=47$ is opposite angle $Q$, and side $QS$ is the hypotenuse. The condition $QR<QS$ is consistent with $QS$ being the longest side.

steps.title: Set up sine
steps.content:
From angle $Q$, sine compares the opposite side to the hypotenuse:
$$
\sin Q=\frac{47}{QS}
$$
This uses the given side $SR=47$ and the requested side $QS$.

steps.title: Solve for the length
steps.content: Multiply both sides by $QS$, then divide by $\sin Q$: $$ QS=\frac{47}{\sin Q} $$ The answer is D.
